rationale: A precision strike will exploit their fraying cohesion and demonstrate resolve, shattering enemy morale while avoiding overextension.
Forward-deploying naval assets in the Cerulean Strait reinforces pressure and secures critical supply lines amid rising neutral complaints.
A rapid, shallow incursion will test defensive gaps and force Tethys to divert resources from urban strongholds to frontier zones.
A targeted cyber attack on Tethys’ command and control infrastructure will exploit their fraying military cohesion and amplify operational confusion amid rising war fatigue.
Covert sabotage of key logistics hubs in occupied territories will degrade their supply capacity and force overextension, complementing our precision military strikes.
Orchestrating a false-flag incident will shift blame for civilian disruptions onto Tethys, eroding international support and undermining their moral standing.
Issuing war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to sustain military and economic pressure, offsetting sanctions' fiscal strain while demonstrating resolve to allies and adversaries alike.
Engaging neutral nations in selective trade negotiations secures critical supply routes and weakens the cohesion of the sanctioning bloc through targeted economic incentives.
Strategically weakening the enemy currency undermines Tethys’ import capacity and erodes public confidence in their economic stability, amplifying internal war fatigue.
Strengthening our frontline positions will exploit Novaris’s stretched logistics and turn stabilized fronts into impenetrable barriers.
Increasing vigilance along contested zones deters infiltration and buys time for fortifications to be completed under cover of fatigue-induced lulls.
Proactive intelligence gathering ensures we detect any false signs of withdrawal and maintain strategic advantage despite war weariness at home.
We must urgently shore up internal defenses to protect our military plans from Novaris’s active infiltration efforts, ensuring our fortification strategies remain secure.
With evidence of escalating cyber operations, reinforcing our critical infrastructure and military networks is essential to maintain operational continuity amid growing digital threats.
Proactively monitoring Novaris’s command structure and supply lines will provide early warning of any renewed offensive moves masked as withdrawal or recalibration.
Issuing war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to extend our economic endurance beyond 14 weeks, directly sustaining war production under severe sanctions.
Engaging neutral countries in targeted trade negotiations can unlock critical supply routes and bypass sanction-induced shortages without compromising sovereignty.
Strategically devaluing our currency will boost export competitiveness of wartime industries and increase revenue from foreign military aid denominated in stronger currencies.
Leveraging our strong international support, I propose convening a formal multilateral summit under UN auspices to institutionalize pressure on Novaris and solidify coalition commitments.
By actively integrating neutral states concerned about strait harassment and cyber spillover into security dialogues, we broaden our diplomatic base and isolate Novaris further.

Deploying international observers to monitor cease-fire lines and humanitarian compliance reinforces our transparency and deters Novaris from exploiting lulls for reconnaissance or false-flag operations.
